message:
Last summer I spent three days without power during a heat wave, and honestly? After the initial panic wore off, my kids rediscovered board games. Like, *actually* played them without phones nearby. We did card tricks, played shadow puppets with flashlights, read books aloud. My teenager even asked to do it again.

Turns out storms have a weird way of reminding us that some of our best entertainment doesn't need WiFi. And now when we think about prepping for severe weather—hurricanes, winter storms, whatever comes—I actually include "games & books we genuinely want to play" on the list alongside flashlights and water.

It sounds small, but staying calm during a weather event is so much easier when you and your family have something to do together that doesn't feel like "surviving."

What's a low-tech activity your family loves? I'm always looking for new ideas!
